outputs.desc: Output Slabs after cutting.

outputs.name: Parent
outputs.desc: Per-output parent index (0-based) into the input Slab list.

outputs.name: TotalVolume
outputs.desc: Sum of signed volumes of all output Slabs (sanity check).

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of resulting Slabs.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Output Slabs as Rhino Meshes (parallel to the Slab list). Wire  into native components (Move, Bake, Boolean, Volume, etc.).

name: Slab Cut By Tool Mesh (CGAL)
description: Cuts a slab/block mesh by an arbitrary tool mesh via CGAL  exact-predicate booleans. Outputs the outside half  (slab − tool), the inside half (slab ∩ tool), or both.  Use this for non-convex slabs or curved/sculpted fracture  tools where the plane-based cutter does not apply.  Implements CGAL PMP corefinement booleans (CGAL_PMP).

algorithm.title: Exact-predicate mesh-mesh CSG via corefinement
algorithm.citation: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing corefine_and_compute_difference/intersection (EPICK+EPECK Hybrid kernel)

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab/block mesh to cut. Must be closed and manifold for  predictable output (run Mesh Repair (CGAL) upstream if in  doubt).

inputs.name: Tool
inputs.desc: Tool mesh used as the cutter. Closed manifold mesh.

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Outside only (slab − tool).  1 = Inside only  (slab ∩ tool).  2 = Both halves (default).

inputs.name: Hybrid Kernel
inputs.desc: True (default) = HYBRID — EPICK storage + EPECK intersection  construction. Robust on near-tangent contacts and multi-cut  chains at a 2–5x speed cost.  False = EPICK only — fastest, fine for well-conditioned inputs.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute. Heavy operation on large inputs.

outputs.name: Outside
outputs.desc: Slab − Tool. The portion of the slab outside the tool.  Empty mesh when the tool fully contains the slab.

outputs.name: Inside
outputs.desc: Slab ∩ Tool. The portion of the slab inside the tool.  Empty mesh when the tool misses the slab entirely.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Which kernel ran: 'CGAL' or 'ManagedBsp' (BSP fallback).

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (mode, kernel, vertex/face counts, runtime).

name: Slab From Mesh
description: Wraps a Rhino mesh into a Slab DTO. Quads stay as quads.  Mesh must have at least 4 vertices and 4 faces. Slab assumes  the input is CONVEX; convexity is not verified here.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Rhino mesh defining a CONVEX polyhedral slab. Quads are  preserved as quads; triangles stay as triangles.

outputs.name: Slab
outputs.desc: Slab DTO. Wire into Slab Cut By Fractures or downstream masonry.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Slab as a Rhino Mesh (re-emitted). Identical geometry to the  input Mesh but fan-triangulated from each polygonal face.

name: Vertical Fracture Planes From Curves
description: Turn plan-view fracture trace curves (e.g. from Vector Fractures  Loader on a real fracture shapefile) into VERTICAL cutting planes  for Slab Cut By Fractures. Per Segment = a plane per polyline segment  (faithful to wiggly traces); off = one best-fit vertical plane per curve.

algorithm.title: Vertical plane per fracture trace
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: plan-view trace -> vertical cutting plane (contains the trace direction + Z)

inputs.name: Curves
inputs.desc: Fracture trace curves (plan-view).

inputs.name: Per Segment
inputs.desc: TRUE = one vertical plane per polyline segment (faithful to curved traces);  FALSE (default) = one best-fit vertical plane per curve (start->end direction).

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Vertical fracture planes (feed Slab Cut By Fractures 'Plane').

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of planes produced.

related.reason: Source of the real fracture trace curves (.shp / .geojson).

related.target: Frahan > Slab > Slab Cut By Fractures
related.reason: Consumes these vertical planes to cut a block into slabs.

name: Frahan Chart Flatness Report
description: Classify per-face area ratios against a flatness threshold.  Threshold is interpreted as max(ratio, 1/ratio); 0.5 and 2.0 are  equally distorted from 1.0. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Per-face area-ratio distortion classification
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Per-Face Area Ratios
inputs.desc: List of per-face area ratios from ChartDistortionAnalyzer.

inputs.name: Threshold
inputs.desc: Flatness threshold (1.0 = no distortion allowed; 1.5 = 50% allowed; 2.0 = 2x).

outputs.name: Total Face Count
outputs.desc: Total faces classified.

outputs.name: Above Threshold Count
outputs.desc: Faces above the threshold.

outputs.name: Above Threshold Ratio
outputs.desc: Above / Total.

outputs.name: Worst Face Index
outputs.desc: Index of the most-distorted face.

outputs.name: Worst Area Ratio
outputs.desc: Normalised distortion of the worst face.

outputs.name: Per-Face Above Flag
outputs.desc: Per-face boolean: true if above threshold.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Single-line summary.

name: Pack On Surface
description: Packs 2D shapes onto a surface chart with the deterministic hole-aware nester (exact NFP  bottom-left-fill, multi-start, 0-overlap), then lifts packed curves to the 3D surface via  barycentric mapping. Runs async: the canvas stays live. [Floater 2003]

algorithm.title: Barycentric 2D-to-3D mapping
algorithm.citation: Floater 2003, Computer Aided Geometric Design 20(1):19-27 Mean value coordinates

inputs.name: Surface Map
inputs.desc: FrahanSurfaceChart from the Surface Chart component.

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar 2D part curves to pack (in the flat chart XY plane).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and between parts and the sheet boundary (model units).

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees. The hole-aware engine uses the COUNT of angles as its  uniform base rotation count (default list 0/90/180/270 -> 4), extended with contact angles.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for the 3D barycentric mapping and containment checks.

inputs.name: Sort Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). It multi-starts over  area/max-dim/width/height orders automatically - see MultiStart.

inputs.name: Corner Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). Placement is always bottom-left-fill.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The engine is deterministic.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The exact NFP enumerates feasible  placements directly.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to True to execute packing. False shows the idle message and cancels any running solve.

inputs.name: ContactRotations
inputs.desc: Longest-edge count per polygon used to build contact (edge-alignment) rotation angles. Default 6.

inputs.name: Resolution
inputs.desc: Solver sampling resolution for smooth part curves (16..200, default 24). Collision proxy only;  packed output is the exact original curve. Solve time grows ~quadratically.

inputs.name: MultiStart
inputs.desc: Deterministic part orders the engine tries, keeping the densest valid layout (1..4, default 4).  1 = single largest-first pass. Higher raises density at ~linear cost, never reduces placements.

outputs.name: Packed 3D
outputs.desc: Packed part curves lifted to the 3D surface.

outputs.name: Packed 2D
outputs.desc: Packed part curves in the flat chart plane (real units).

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Curves that could not be placed in the chart.

outputs.name: Failed 3D
outputs.desc: Number of packed curves that failed 3D barycentric mapping (likely cross a UV seam).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Packing and mapping report.

name: Pack Surfaces
description: Packs 2D shapes across one or more surface charts with the deterministic hole-aware  nester (exact NFP bottom-left-fill, multi-start, 0-overlap), then maps them onto the 3D  surfaces. Runs async: the canvas stays live and the result pops in when ready. Outputs  Full Transform to place original flat parts on the surface without distortion.

inputs.name: Surface Maps
inputs.desc: One or more FrahanSurfaceChart objects from the Surface Chart component. Each becomes one  sheet (greedy overflow chart 0 -> chart 1 -> ...).

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar 2D part curves to pack.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and chart boundaries (model units).

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees. The hole-aware engine uses the COUNT of angles as its  uniform base rotation count (default list 0/90/180/270 -> 4) and extends it with  contact (edge-alignment) angles. Pass more angles to raise the base count.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for the 3D barycentric mapping and containment checks.

inputs.name: Sort Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The engine multi-starts over  area/max-dim/width/height orders automatically and keeps the best - see MultiStart.

inputs.name: Corner Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). Placement is always bottom-left-fill.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The engine is deterministic:  identical inputs always reproduce the same layout.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The exact NFP enumerates feasible  placements directly.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to True to execute packing. False shows the idle message and cancels any running solve.

inputs.name: ContactRotations
inputs.desc: Longest-edge count per polygon used to build contact (edge-alignment) rotation angles so  parts seat flush. Default 6.

inputs.name: Resolution
inputs.desc: Solver sampling resolution for smooth part curves (16..200, default 24). This only sets the  collision proxy - packed output is always the exact original curve. Solve time grows  ~quadratically; raise only for tight concave notches.

inputs.name: MultiStart
inputs.desc: Deterministic part orders the engine tries per chart, keeping the densest valid layout  (1..4, default 4). 1 = single largest-first pass. Higher raises density at ~linear cost and  never reduces placements or validity.

outputs.name: Packed 3D
outputs.desc: Packed curves lifted to the 3D surface via barycentric mapping (shape follows surface).

outputs.name: Placement Planes
outputs.desc: Rigid placement frame on the 3D surface per packed part.  Origin = centroid on surface, X/Y = surface tangent axes, Z = surface normal.

outputs.name: Transforms 3D
outputs.desc: Transform from PACKED 2D position to the 3D surface placement frame.  Apply to Packed 2D curves to get rigid (non-deformed) parts on the surface.

outputs.name: Full Transform
outputs.desc: Composed transform: original flat part -> 3D surface in one step.  Apply to the ORIGINAL part geometry (before packing) using Part Index to select it.

outputs.name: Max Deviation
outputs.desc: Maximum gap (model units) between the flat part and the curved surface  at the four bounding-box corners. Small = nearly flat. Large = needs shimming.

outputs.name: Packed 2D
outputs.desc: Packed curves in each chart's native coordinate space.

outputs.name: Chart Index
outputs.desc: Which Surface Map (0-based) each packed part was placed on.

outputs.name: Part Index
outputs.desc: 0-based index into the original Parts input list for each packed part.  Use with List Item to select the matching original part, then apply Full Transform.

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Curves that could not be placed on any chart.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Packing and mapping report.

name: Surface Chart
description: Unwraps a 3D mesh to a 2D UV chart using Boundary-First Flattening (BFF).  BFF must be downloaded separately and the exe path provided as input. [Sawhney & Crane 2017]

algorithm.title: BFF boundary-first flattening
algorithm.citation: Sawhney and Crane 2017, ACM TOG 36(4):109

inputs.name: Surface
inputs.desc: Mesh to unwrap. Accepts any Rhino mesh — cleaned automatically before BFF.

inputs.name: BFF Exe Path
inputs.desc: Optional. Path to bff-command-line.exe.  Leave unconnected to auto-detect next to the .gha file.

inputs.name: Cones
inputs.desc: Number of cone singularities (0 = auto, 1–8 for complex surfaces).

inputs.name: Normalize UVs
inputs.desc: Normalize output UVs to [0,1]. Required for chart scale computation.

inputs.name: Timeout (s)
inputs.desc: Maximum seconds to wait for BFF before aborting.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to True to execute unwrapping.

outputs.name: Flat Mesh
outputs.desc: 2D unwrapped mesh in UV space (Z=0 plane). Scale by ChartScale for real dimensions.

outputs.name: Surface Map
outputs.desc: FrahanSurfaceChart object. Wire into the Pack On Surface component.

outputs.name: Boundary
outputs.desc: Outer boundary polyline of the flat chart scaled to real units.

outputs.name: Distortion
outputs.desc: Max/min edge stretch ratio. Values far from 1.0 indicate mapping distortion.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Quality metrics, warnings, and timing.

name: Frahan Trencadís Catalog Pack
description: Trencadís catalog packer: partition each sheet into CVD-Lloyd  cells, then optimally assign catalog parts to cells via the  Hungarian algorithm. Best when piece count matches target  coverage and you want each piece placed exactly once.

algorithm.title: CVD-Lloyd interior seeding
algorithm.citation: Lloyd 1982 centroidal Voronoi diagram relaxation

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Catalog of irregular shard curves to place. Each piece will be  placed exactly once.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. Branch {0} = sheet 0, etc.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to run the catalog assignment.

inputs.name: Lloyd Iterations
inputs.desc: CVD-Lloyd relaxation iterations. Higher = more uniform cells.

inputs.name: Grout
inputs.desc: Inward offset applied to each piece AFTER trim, to leave the  characteristic trencadís mortar gap. 0 = no grout. Default 0.02.

outputs.name: Placed Pieces
outputs.desc: Catalog parts placed at their assigned cell centroids.

outputs.name: Cell Seeds
outputs.desc: CVD-Lloyd seed centroids — one per assigned cell.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Catalog index for each placed piece.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet index for each placed piece.

outputs.name: Cell Areas
outputs.desc: Approximate area of each assigned cell. Useful to spot  outliers where the piece is much smaller / bigger than the  cell.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Catalog packing report.

name: Frahan Trencadís Dynamic Settle
description: Light Kangaroo 2 settle for trimmed trencadís packing.  Each piece is one centroid particle. SphereCollide pushes  overlapping centroids apart, Anchor pulls back to post-  packing centroid, OnCurve sticks boundary-adjacent pieces  to the sheet edge. Pieces translate rigidly so shape and  edge lengths are exactly preserved.

algorithm.title: Trencadis dynamic settle
algorithm.citation: F-2D-002.F8 Frahan-original

inputs.name: Pieces
inputs.desc: Trimmed pieces from a trencadís packer. Centroid of each  input curve becomes the anchor target — i.e. the answer  point produced by the boundary packing algorithm upstream.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet outlines. OnCurve targets.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. OnCurve targets too.

inputs.name: Apply Physics
inputs.desc: Master toggle. False = pure pass-through (output = input).  True = solve.

inputs.name: Iterations
inputs.desc: Maximum solver step count. Solver early-exits when kinetic  energy drops below 1e-6. Default 100.

inputs.name: Anchor Strength
inputs.desc: Pull strength on each centroid back toward its post-packing  position. 0 = disabled. Default 0.05.

inputs.name: Collide Strength
inputs.desc: SphereCollide goal strength. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Boundary Pull
inputs.desc: OnCurve goal strength for centroids within 1.5× mean radius  of any boundary curve. 0 = disabled. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Collide Radius Factor
inputs.desc: Multiplier on the mean per-piece bounding radius used as  the SphereCollide radius. >1 = more space between pieces.  <1 = pieces can overlap (centroid distance allowed to be  less than mean radius). Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Strict Containment
inputs.desc: Hard per-vertex boundary collider. When True, after each  Kangaroo step the proposed centroid translation is binary- searched to find the largest fraction that keeps EVERY  vertex of the piece inside at least one Sheet Outline and  outside every Sheet Hole. Pieces whose initial vertices  are already outside the boundary won't move (fraction = 0).  When False, only the soft OnCurve boundary pull on centroids  is applied (legacy behaviour). Default True.

outputs.name: Settled Pieces
outputs.desc: Pieces translated by (settledCentroid − originalCentroid).  Shape and edge lengths preserved exactly via rigid translation.

outputs.name: Translations
outputs.desc: Per-piece translation vector applied during settle.

outputs.name: Final Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-piece centroid after settling.

outputs.name: Final vSum
outputs.desc: Final kinetic-energy sum. < 1e-6 indicates well converged.

outputs.name: Residual Overlap
outputs.desc: Sum of polygon-pair intersection areas after settle. This  is the dynamic tolerance — how much overlap remains in the  tolerance-based packing once Kangaroo has resolved as much  as it can. 0 = clean fit.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Settle report.

name: Frahan Trencadís EdgeMatch
description: Trencadís packer driven by the EdgeMatch beam-search solver.  Each sheet outline becomes an anchored frame; parts are placed  by their complementary edges against the frame and against  previously-placed parts. Output is deterministic for fixed  input order.

algorithm.title: EdgeMatch-powered Trencadis pack
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original alternative to Battiato 2013 CVD+GVF stack

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar shard curves to pack.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed sheet boundary curves. Each becomes an anchored frame  for one independent EdgeMatch run.

inputs.name: Joint Width
inputs.desc: Allowed mean edge-to-edge gap (document units). Mapped onto  EdgeMatch's residual threshold: matches further than this are  rejected. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Sample Spacing
inputs.desc: Arc-length sample spacing along each contour. Match scanner  resolution.

inputs.name: Break Angle
inputs.desc: Curvature break-point threshold in degrees per window.

inputs.name: Min Segment Length
inputs.desc: Below this chord length a segment is treated as noise.

inputs.name: Beam Width
inputs.desc: Concurrent beam states retained per iteration. 16 recommended  for Trencadís (more local minima than wood).

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Outer-loop iteration cap.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Execute the solver.

inputs.name: Non-Crossing
inputs.desc: Order-preserving rim correspondence. FALSE (default) = free  nearest-point ICP (unchanged behaviour). TRUE = monotone,  non-crossing point pairing between shard edges; more robust on  wiggly / noisy fracture edges where free matching tangles.

outputs.name: Placed Pieces
outputs.desc: Shard contours transformed into their solved placements.  Sheet outlines are not included (they are the identity frame).

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-piece rigid transform.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original Parts list index for each placed piece.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet list index this piece was placed onto.

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Source-curve copies of parts that did not find a match on any sheet.

outputs.name: Residuals
outputs.desc: Per-placement ICP residual.

outputs.name: Total Residual
outputs.desc: Sum of all per-placement residuals across all sheets.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable per-sheet summary.

name: Frahan Trencadís Pack
description: Trencadís ('broken-tile') 2D mosaic packer. Places irregular  pieces with bounded overlap, then boolean-differences  the overlapping bits so pieces butt edge-to-edge with  characteristic chipped fits. Optional grout offset leaves  the mortar gap. SKELETON — returns empty result.

algorithm.title: Trencadis greedy pack basic
algorithm.citation: Gaudi Park Guell broken-tile mosaic technique

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar shard curves to pack. Irregular shapes welcome —  trencadís is at its best with non-uniform pieces.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves. The mosaic is built  inside these.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. Branch {0} = sheet 0, {1} = sheet 1, etc.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Pre-trim part-to-part clearance. The trim post-pass removes  everything inside this clearance, so think of it as the  MAXIMUM grout gap (actual gap = Grout, see below).

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees. Default 0/45/90/135 to  encourage varied edge orientation typical of trencadís work.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for containment / collision / boolean  difference.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic; non-zero changes tie-breaking randomisation  of placement order.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to true to execute packing.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: Candidate budget per part per rotation. Trencadís typically  needs MORE candidates than V506 because trim acceptance  expands the feasible set. 0 = default (600).

inputs.name: Trim Tolerance
inputs.desc: Maximum allowed part-to-part overlap depth (document units)  during placement. Larger = more aggressive chipping, denser  pack. Default 0.2; for meter-scale 0.1–1.0.

inputs.name: Grout
inputs.desc: Inward offset applied to each piece AFTER trim, to leave the  characteristic trencadís mortar gap. 0 = no grout (raw  edge-to-edge). Default 0.02.

inputs.name: Boundary Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = off (interior fill only).  1 = boundary-aware bias: shards with edges matching the sheet  or hole edges are placed first AND auto-rotated to align with  the matched boundary tangent. All candidate sources used.  2 = strict two-phase ring/interior — boundary-worthy shards  use only boundary-anchor candidates first (true ring), then  non-boundary shards fill the interior. Falls back to all  candidates if a phase is saturated.  3 = uniform curve division — divide each boundary curve by  arc length; place each shard with longest edge tangent to  the curve at its assigned position.

inputs.name: Min Boundary Affinity
inputs.desc: Edge-match score at or above which a shard is considered  boundary-worthy. Range [0, 1]. Only applies when Boundary  Mode > 0.

inputs.name: Cut Budget
inputs.desc: Battiato 2013 §4 cumulative-cut cap as a fraction of each  shard's area. T_N = budget on a NEW shard's total chipping  across all neighbours; T_P = budget on a PLACED shard  (derived as Cut/2); single-cut caps S_N (Cut/2) and S_P  (Cut/4) cap any one chip. Default 0.35 matches Battiato's  recommended T_N. Lower → less aggressive chipping, more  shard-shape preservation. 0 → no cuts allowed (strict  no-overlap; defeats the trencadís technique).

inputs.name: Use CVD Seeds
inputs.desc: Initialize per-sheet placement using CVD-Lloyd seed points  (blue-noise distribution). Improves coverage uniformity vs  the bbox-corner default starting point.

inputs.name: Use GVF Orientation
inputs.desc: Compute Gradient Vector Flow over each sheet to bias shard  rotation toward the local boundary tangent. Pieces follow  curves like Gaudí's columns. Slower than the discrete  rotation list alone but produces the flow-line look.

inputs.name: GVF Smoothness
inputs.desc: GVF μ (smoothness regularizer). Lower (0.05–0.15) → field  follows boundary closely, sharper. Higher (0.3–0.5) →  smoother propagation into interior. Default 0.2 (Battiato  2008).

outputs.name: Trencadís Pieces
outputs.desc: Final shard curves after trim + grout. Use these for  downstream rendering / fabrication.

outputs.name: Pre-Trim Pieces
outputs.desc: Placed shards BEFORE the trim post-pass. Useful for  debugging which piece chipped which.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Placement transforms (per source curve).

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original input curve index for each placed shard.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet index used for each placed shard.

outputs.name: Trim Adjacency
outputs.desc: DataTree per shard: branch i lists the SOURCE indices of  earlier-placed shards that chipped Trencadís Pieces[i].

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Shards that could not be placed even with trim tolerance.

outputs.name: Failure Reasons
outputs.desc: Reason for each unplaced shard.

outputs.name: Sheet Preview
outputs.desc: Outer sheet and hole preview curves.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Trencadís packing report (counts, timings, trim events).

name: Frahan Trencadís Pipeline
description: All-in-one trencadís pipeline. Deterministic boundary  pack first; if residual overlap remains, Kangaroo 2  settle fills the gaps. Exposes solver controls (kinetic  energy threshold, momentum) for cases where the  deterministic pass alone is insufficient.

algorithm.title: Trencadis greedy pack
algorithm.citation: Gaudi Park Guell broken-tile mosaic technique

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar shard curves to pack.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet outlines.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Master toggle. False = no output.

inputs.name: Apply Physics
inputs.desc: Toggle for the dynamic settle stage. Right-click → Show  Physics Tuning to expose strength / convergence / momentum  controls.

inputs.name: Live Animate
inputs.desc: Step-by-step animated settle with viewport overlay. Right- click → Show Animation Tuning to expose frame controls.

outputs.name: Settled Pieces
outputs.desc: Final pieces.

outputs.name: Packed Pieces
outputs.desc: Boundary-packed pieces BEFORE physics.

outputs.name: Translations
outputs.desc: Per-piece translation applied during settle.

outputs.name: Final Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-piece centroid after the pipeline.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original input curve index per placed shard.

outputs.name: Final vSum
outputs.desc: Final kinetic-energy sum.

outputs.name: Residual Overlap
outputs.desc: Sum of polygon-pair intersection areas after pipeline.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Pipeline report.

outputs.name: Pre-Trim Pieces
outputs.desc: Pre-trim placed curves directly out of the boundary  packer (TrencadisFill.PackedCurves). One curve per  placed source. Compare against Packed Pieces (Cp) to see  what was trimmed off near the sheet edge / hole edges.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-placed-piece rigid transform from the source curve's  frame to its world placement. One Transform per piece,  parallel to Packed Pieces (Cp) and Source Indices (Src).  Apply to any source-frame geometry (drill points, hatch  patterns) to bring it into the placed frame.

outputs.name: Trim Adjacency
outputs.desc: Per-piece tree where branch {i} holds the source-indices  of OTHER pieces that trimmed piece i during the deterministic  boundary pass. Empty branch = piece i was not trimmed  against any other piece. Useful for auditing chain-cut  relationships in trencadís layouts.

name: Arch Voussoirs
description: Generate a stereotomic arch as N radial voussoir cells (8-vertex  wedge solids; bed joints normal to the intrados). Profiles:  Semicircular / Segmental / Pointed / Catenary. Outputs the cut-stone  cells plus a typed VoussoirAssembly for Voussoir Stone Matcher + the  rubble match-and-trim (example 21). Grounded in  wiki/research/stereotomy_voussoir_from_rubble.md.

algorithm.title: RadialVoussoirCells
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: intrados curve -> arc-length stations -> 8-vertex wedge solids with radial bed joints (extrados = intrados offset by ring thickness along the outward normal)

inputs.name: Profile
inputs.desc: Intrados profile: 0 = Semicircular, 1 = Segmental, 2 = Pointed  (equilateral), 3 = Catenary. Default 0.

inputs.name: Intrados Radius
inputs.desc: Intrados radius (m). For Catenary this is the span. Default 2.0.

inputs.name: Ring Thickness
inputs.desc: Radial thickness intrados->extrados (m). Default 0.55.

inputs.name: Width
inputs.desc: Out-of-plane voussoir width (m). Default 0.6.

inputs.name: Count
inputs.desc: Number of voussoirs. Default 11.

inputs.name: Included Angle
inputs.desc: Included angle for the Segmental profile (deg, 0..180). Ignored by  the other profiles. Default 120.

inputs.name: Rise
inputs.desc: Apex rise for the Catenary profile (m). 0 = use Intrados Radius.  Ignored by the other profiles. Default 0.

inputs.name: Base Point
inputs.desc: Origin to translate the arch to (built in world XZ, width along Y,  springers on z=0). Default world origin.

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: The voussoir cut-stone cells (closed wedge solids), in install order.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: Typed VoussoirAssembly. Wire into Voussoir Stone Matcher (D5F10010).

outputs.name: Bed Planes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir lower bed-joint plane (radial). The springer's is the springing plane.

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir centroid.

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir volume (m^3).

outputs.name: Keystone
outputs.desc: Index of the keystone voussoir (nearest the apex).

outputs.name: Intrados
outputs.desc: The intrados (soffit) curve.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Build summary (profile, span, rise, total volume, closedness).

name: Pendentive Vault Voussoirs
description: Generate a pendentive (sail) dome (sphere over a square) tessellated  on a grid into voussoir cells along the sphere's lines of curvature,  each extruded radially by the shell thickness. Outputs the cut-stone  cells plus a typed VoussoirAssembly for Voussoir Stone Matcher + the  rubble match-and-trim (example 22). Grounded in  wiki/research/stereotomy_voussoir_from_rubble.md.

algorithm.title: PendentiveDomeCells
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: square plan grid lifted onto a sphere (z=sqrt(R^2-x^2-y^2)) then extruded radially by the shell thickness -> 8-vertex cells along lines of curvature

inputs.name: Sphere Radius
inputs.desc: Sphere radius (m). Default 2.5.

inputs.name: Square Half Width
inputs.desc: Half the side of the square plan (m). Must satisfy 2*h^2 < R^2 so the  corners lie on the sphere. Default 1.6.

inputs.name: Shell Thickness
inputs.desc: Radial shell thickness intrados->extrados (m). Default 0.4.

inputs.name: Grid U
inputs.desc: Cells across the plan in U. Default 6.

inputs.name: Grid V
inputs.desc: Cells across the plan in V. Default 6.

inputs.name: Drop To Ground
inputs.desc: Translate so the springing corners rest on z=0. Default true.

inputs.name: Base Point
inputs.desc: Origin to translate the vault to. Default world origin.

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: The voussoir cut-stone cells (closed solids), one per grid cell.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: Typed VoussoirAssembly. Wire into Voussoir Stone Matcher (D5F10010).

outputs.name: Bed Planes
outputs.desc: Per-cell intrados (bed) plane: centre + outward sphere radial.

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-cell centroid.

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-cell volume (m^3).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Build summary (R, span, thickness, grid, springing/apex, total volume).

name: Voussoir Ingest
description: Read a list of voussoir meshes (from the Voussoir GH plugin or  Frahan Stereotomic Vault Mode) as a typed VoussoirAssembly.  Per-voussoir record carries OBB + volume + centroid + bed/head  planes + load axis + joint class. Emits MatchItem[] for downstream  MatcherContextBuilder (the substrate spine). First step of the  top-down voussoir-to-stone workflow per philosophy doc §10.6.

algorithm.title: VoussoirIngestPipeline
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original Voussoir GH plugin -> typed VoussoirAssembly DTO

inputs.name: Voussoirs
inputs.desc: List of voussoir geometries representing the designed stereotomic  assembly. Accepts EITHER Mesh OR Brep. The Voussoir GH plugin by  Varela (FAUP Porto STBIM) emits BREP per voussoir as a GH data  tree -- this component handles both natively. Closed solids  preferred; Breps are meshed via Mesh.CreateFromBrep at default  MeshingParameters quality.

inputs.name: Joint Classes
inputs.desc: Optional per-voussoir position-role tags: 'bed' / 'head' / 'key' /  'ground' / 'void' (default 'void'). Same count as Voussoirs (or  empty / single-value for default).

inputs.name: Thrust Curve
inputs.desc: Optional funicular thrust curve (from TNA form-finding or hand  drafting). Drives LoadAxis per voussoir via closest-point-tangent.

inputs.name: Lithology Hints
inputs.desc: Optional per-voussoir lithology constraint (e.g. 'Vermont Marble').  Used as a categorical constraint in the matcher.

inputs.name: Ground Anchor Indices
inputs.desc: Optional indices of springer / abutment voussoirs (start points  of the install DAG). Empty = auto-detect via lowest centroid Z.

inputs.name: Adjacency Threshold
inputs.desc: Fraction of face-diagonal for adjacency detection. Default 0.05  (5% of object span). Faces within this distance count as a shared joint.

inputs.name: Provenance
inputs.desc: Optional provenance string for the assembly (e.g. 'Voussoir plugin v2.3 output').

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: The typed VoussoirAssembly. Wire into VoussoirStoneMatcher +  VoussoirPackIntoBlock downstream.

outputs.name: Match Items
outputs.desc: List of MatchItem (substrate-compatible). Wire into MatcherContextBuilder  as the Demand side. Numeric props: Volume, MaxDim, MinDim, Height.  Categorical: JointClass, LithologyHint.

outputs.name: OBBs
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir oriented bounding boxes (AABB v1).

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir mesh volume (absolute).

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir geometric centroid.

outputs.name: Bed Planes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir bed-joint plane (largest-area face heuristic v1).

outputs.name: Head Planes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir head-joint plane (second-largest-area face heuristic v1).

outputs.name: Load Axes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir compressive-load direction (thrust-curve tangent if  supplied, else OBB longest-axis).

outputs.name: Adjacency Pairs
outputs.desc: Pairs of voussoir indices that share a joint face (flat list:  [i0, j0, i1, j1, ...]).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir + assembly-level diagnostic notes.

name: Voussoir Pack Into Block
description: Pack as many voussoirs as possible into a single quarried block.  Greedy first-fit-decreasing on AABB extents (v1; v2 routes through  BlockPackTree DLBF + CGAL exact-shape fit). Outputs: placed  voussoir indices + per-voussoir transforms + cut-plane plan +  achieved yield ratio. Use case: extract all voussoirs of a vault  from one large quarry block (Quarra Two Horse Relief pattern).

algorithm.title: Greedy first-fit-decreasing 3D bin-pack
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original v1 packing; foundational reference: Garey-Johnson 1979 FFD

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: VoussoirAssembly from VoussoirIngestComponent (D5F1000F).

inputs.name: Block
inputs.desc: A single quarried block (accepts QuarryBlock typed record from  ScanToBlockInventoryComponent F2D0BC20, OR a raw Mesh).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Gap between adjacent voussoirs (mm), used as the saw-kerf +  carving allowance. Default 5.0 mm.

inputs.name: Grid Step
inputs.desc: Grid step for the candidate-position scan (mm). Smaller = denser  search = slower. Default 25.0 mm.

inputs.name: Allow Skip
inputs.desc: If true, voussoirs that cannot be placed are skipped + reported.  If false, fail loudly when any voussoir cannot be placed. Default true.

outputs.name: Placed Voussoirs
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir transformed mesh (placed in the block's local frame).  Null where the voussoir could not be placed.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir placement transform (identity where unplaced).

outputs.name: Fit Voussoir Indices
outputs.desc: Indices of voussoirs that were successfully placed (sorted by  placement order = volume descending).

outputs.name: Skip Voussoir Indices
outputs.desc: Indices of voussoirs that could NOT be placed (under-provisioned).

outputs.name: Yield Ratio
outputs.desc: Sum-of-placed-volumes / block-volume. >= 0.4 is typically  production-acceptable per UCL Devadass 2025 §2.7.

outputs.name: Cut Planes
outputs.desc: Cut-plan: one plane per adjacent-voussoir-pair joint inside the  block. The cutting sequence is implied by placement order.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Diagnostic notes -- voussoir count placed/skipped, yield,  block fill rate.

name: Voussoir Stone Matcher
description: Assign each voussoir to a quarry stone via Kuhn 1955 Hungarian  bipartite assignment. Voussoirs are demand; stones are supply;  feasibility = stone OBB contains voussoir OBB + safety margin +  yield_ratio >= MinYield; cost = w_yield * (1 - yield_ratio) +  w_carving * (carving_vol / voussoir_vol). The canonical top-down  voussoir-to-stone matcher per wiki/research/voussoir_stereotomy_integration.md  Phase 2 + philosophy doc §10.6. First production use of the  MatcherRegistry substrate.

algorithm.title: Kuhn1955Hungarian
algorithm.citation: H.W. Kuhn 1955 Hungarian Method for the Assignment Problem; Naval Research Logistics Quarterly 2:83-97; Jonker-Volgenant pivot

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: VoussoirAssembly from VoussoirIngestComponent (D5F1000F).

inputs.name: Quarry Stones
inputs.desc: List of quarry-block candidates. Accepts either:  (a) QuarryBlock typed records from ScanToBlockInventoryComponent  (F2D0BC20), or (b) raw Mesh inputs (in which case AABB+volume  are computed inline). Mixed lists are accepted.

inputs.name: Min Yield
inputs.desc: Minimum yield ratio (voussoir_vol / stone_vol) for a feasible pair.  Default 0.4 (40%). Stones below this are excluded as wasteful.

inputs.name: Safety Margin
inputs.desc: Safety margin added to voussoir OBB extent before containment test  (mm). Default 5.0.

inputs.name: Yield Weight
inputs.desc: Cost weight for the yield term `1 - yield_ratio`. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Carving Weight
inputs.desc: Cost weight for the carving term `(stone_vol - voussoir_vol) / voussoir_vol`.  Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Allow Empty
inputs.desc: If true, unassigned voussoirs are reported (under-provisioned case).  If false, fail loudly when any voussoir would remain unassigned.  Default true.

outputs.name: Assignment
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir stone index (-1 = unassigned).

outputs.name: Placed Stones
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir assigned stone mesh (null where unassigned).

outputs.name: Yield Ratios
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir yield ratio (voussoir_vol / stone_vol). 0 if unassigned.

outputs.name: Carving Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir carving volume (stone_vol - voussoir_vol). 0 if unassigned.

outputs.name: Per-Pair Cost
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir total cost (yield + carving weighted sum).  +Inf where unassigned / infeasible.

outputs.name: Unassigned Voussoirs
outputs.desc: Indices of voussoirs that received no stone (under-provisioned).

outputs.name: Unused Stones
outputs.desc: Indices of stones that were not consumed.

outputs.name: Total Cost
outputs.desc: Sum of per-pair costs across the assignment.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Diagnostic notes -- strategy, infeasibility reasons, M*N matrix size.
